raw_extraction.innovation: A unified multimodal transformer architecture trained on diverse tasks (vision, language, control) to achieve zero-shot generalization across domains.
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Combination of text, vision, and control data (including Atari, Meta-World, BabyAI, DM Control Suite, RGB Stacking, image-text pairs, and dialogue)
raw_extraction.application: Simulated control tasks (Atari, Meta-World, BabyAI, DM Control Suite), robotics (RGB Stacking), image captioning, dialogue systems, and multimodal reasoning.
raw_extraction.license: Closed source (not explicitly stated, but typical for DeepMind research papers)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Developing a generalist agent capable of performing a wide range of tasks with minimal task-specific fine-tuning.
raw_extraction.pretraining_task: Multimodal multimodal next-token prediction (text, vision, control)
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Fine-tuning on task-specific demonstrations for adaptation to new tasks
raw_extraction.extension: Uses stochastic depth regularization (skip layers with 10% probability) during pretraining

raw_extraction.innovation: Scaled-up version of Gato architecture with improved performance through larger capacity, demonstrating stronger scaling laws in multimodal generalization.
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Combination of text, vision, and control data (same as 79M and 1.18B variants)
raw_extraction.application: Same as 79M variant, plus detailed analysis of few-shot performance and out-of-distribution adaptation.
raw_extraction.research_problem: Evaluating the benefits of model scaling in a generalist agent framework.
raw_extraction.pretraining_task: Multimodal multimodal next-token prediction (text, vision, control)
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Fine-tuning on task-specific demonstrations for adaptation to new tasks
raw_extraction.extension: Uses stochastic depth regularization (skip layers with 10% probability) during pretraining

raw_extraction.innovation: Largest variant of Gato, showcasing superior few-shot adaptation and out-of-distribution performance through increased model capacity.
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Combination of text, vision, and control data (same as 79M and 364M variants)
raw_extraction.application: All applications of smaller variants, with emphasis on robotics (real-world RGB Stacking), high-performance few-shot learning, and complex multimodal reasoning.
raw_extraction.research_problem: Exploring the limits of multimodal generalization in large-scale models.
raw_extraction.pretraining_task: Multimodal multimodal next-token prediction (text, vision, control)
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Fine-tuning on task-specific demonstrations for adaptation to new tasks
raw_extraction.extension: Uses stochastic depth regularization (skip layers with 10% probability) during pretraining; optimized for low-latency inference in real-time robotic applications

raw_extraction.innovation: **Generalist agent** combining vision, language, and robotics in a single unified model via cross-modal pretraining, enabling zero-shot generalization across diverse tasks without task-specific architectures.
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Internal proprietary datasets including web text, images, videos, Atari games, simulated robotics environments, and real-world robot interactions
raw_extraction.application: Vision-language grounding, robotic manipulation, game playing, natural language processing, and embodied AI agents
raw_extraction.research_problem: Developing a single model capable of performing a wide range of perception, language, and control tasks with minimal task-specific adaptation.
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Multi-task fine-tuning across vision, language, and robotics tasks using supervised learning and reinforcement learning signals
raw_extraction.extension: Cross-modal attention mechanisms integrating visual, textual, and motor control signals within a single transformer backbone.
